Geological Periods.description: Cambrian explosion: rapid diversification of marine life.
Marine diversification and first land plants.
Development of land plants and fish.
Age of fishes and appearance of first tetrapods.
Swamp forests and formation of major coal deposits.
Formation of Pangaea and diversification of reptiles.
Ice ages and the emergence of modern humans.

Quaternary.description: Ice ages and the emergence of modern humans.

Biological Groups.description: Diversification after the extinction of the dinosaurs.
Emergence of humans and evolution of the genus Homo.

Biological Innovations.description: Explosive diversification over a few million years: appearance of complex eyes, primitive jaws, exoskeletons and nervous systems.
Haikouichthys and Myllokunmingia (China) — first animals with a notochord and a rudimentary skull.
Colonization of the continents by vegetation (bryophytes, then vascular plants) — radical transformation of landscapes.
Appearance of jawed vertebrates — a feeding and predatory revolution opening new ecological niches.

Biological Innovations.titre: First Tetrapods — Out of the Water
Biological Innovations.description: Tiktaalik, Acanthostega — aquatic-terrestrial transition, first limbs with digits, beginnings of aerial respiration.
Appearance of the first insects capable of flight during the Carboniferous — an unprecedented innovation in the history of life.
Key innovation allowing reproduction out of water — amniotic membrane protecting the embryo, freeing reptiles from the aquatic environment.
Appearance in the Late Triassic — they will dominate terrestrial life for 165 million years.
Pterosaurs are the first vertebrates to develop active flapping flight, long before birds.

Biological Innovations.titre: First Mammals and Appearance of Hair
Biological Innovations.description: Small nocturnal mammals descended from therapsids — thermoregulation through hair, nursing of young.
Archaeopteryx (Late Jurassic) — evolutionary link between theropod dinosaurs and modern birds; first true feathers.
Explosive radiation of angiosperms — co-evolution with pollinating insects, transformation of terrestrial ecosystems.
Appearance of the ancestors of monkeys, great apes and hominids.

Extinctions.description: First major mass extinction — disappearance of approximately 85% of marine species.
Major biological crisis affecting marine ecosystems and fish.
The greatest extinction in history: ~90% of marine species and ~70% of terrestrial species disappear.

Extinctions.titre: Cretaceous-Paleogene Extinction — End of the Dinosaurs
Extinctions.description: Impact of the Chicxulub asteroid — disappearance of non-avian dinosaurs, ecological niches freed for mammals.
Disappearance of large mammals such as the mammoth.

Hominids.description: Last common ancestor between the human lineage and that of chimpanzees.
Permanent upright walking — frees the hands for tool use, adaptation to African savannas.
First anatomically modern humans, in Africa.
Migration of Homo sapiens out of Africa and gradual peopling of the world.
Domestication of plants and animals, settlement of populations.
End of the last glaciation, current epoch.
